evidence: ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks is a clean single-pass state machine: all mutable state (inSentence, sentencesInChunk, writer) is local and explicitly bounded; try-finally ensures the writer is closed even on exceptions.

BlackLabSnippetParser uses 8 pre-compiled static Pattern constants; each public/package-private method is <20 lines and does exactly one thing (e.g. trimLeftAtSentenceBoundary, extractCollocateLemma).

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern decomposes into four aptly-named private helpers (fetchSeedCollocates, buildNounProfiles, scoreNouns, identifyCoreCollocates), each with a single bounded phase concern.
ExplorationHandlers.buildExploreResponseBody and buildBaseExploreResponse correctly extract the shared response-construction code used by both single-seed and multi-seed handlers.

scoreHits (BlackLabQueryExecutor, line ~257) uses 'int limit = Math.min(records.size(), maxResults * 3)' — the 3x multiplier is an undocumented over-fetch heuristic; why 3x vs 2x or 5x is invisible to the reader.

parseExploreParams covers top/min_shared/min_logdice but handleSemanticFieldExplore has its own ad-hoc try/catch block to parse nouns_per (line ~71-76) outside the helper, breaking the extraction symmetry between the two explore handlers.

GrammarConfigLoader primary constructor (lines 97-192, ~95 lines) performs JSON parsing, schema validation (version, copulas check, relations array), per-relation position derivation and position range validation, and list/map construction all in one body without delegating to named sub-methods.

strengths: ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks is a textbook single-pass state machine: all mutable state is local, side effects are bounded, and resource cleanup is correctly placed in finally.
BlackLabSnippetParser is a model of micro-method discipline: pre-compiled regex constants, uniform null/empty guards, package-private visibility, each method <20 lines with a single concern.

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern decomposes a multi-step algorithm into four aptly-named private helpers, each corresponding to a single algorithm phase — a developer can understand any phase in isolation.

ExplorationHandlers extracts buildExploreResponseBody and buildBaseExploreResponse to eliminate duplication between the single-seed and multi-seed response paths, showing active management of local duplication.
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens is a depth-counting bracket walker with inline examples in its Javadoc; the logic is proportionate and intention-revealing.
low_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are sparse but recurring: an undocumented heuristic constant in a phase method, an incomplete extraction in a shared helper, and a constructor that does too many things without sub-method delegation — all small, but they share the root cause of incomplete local refactoring.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase reaches well above the 70 anchor: control flow is generally direct, phase decomposition in the exploration code is genuinely clean, and micro-methods in parser utilities are precise. However, three persistent rough edges — an unexplained 3x multiplier in scoreHits, the asymmetric ExploreParams helper, and the 95-line GrammarConfigLoader constructor — together prevent a 80+ rating. These are not isolated one-offs; they appear in the most-used query path and the primary config class, so a developer working in these areas will encounter them regularly. A score of 76 reflects solid craft with a pattern of near-but-not-quite completed extractions.

issues.summary: Unexplained 3x over-fetch heuristic in scoreHits makes the phase limit opaque
evidence: Line ~257 in scoreHits: 'int limit = Math.min(records.size(), maxResults * 3);' — the 3x multiplier is a silent heuristic. Phase 3 (executeBcqlQuery) then re-sorts and limits to maxResults, but the reader has no way to reason about why 3x ensures enough candidates survive scoring without either reading the caller or guessing.
issues.suggestion: Add a named constant or inline comment explaining the over-fetch ratio, e.g.: 'private static final int SCORING_OVERSAMPLE_FACTOR = 3; // score 3x candidates to absorb logDice=0 hits and ensure maxResults survive the final sort' and use SCORING_OVERSAMPLE_FACTOR in scoreHits.

issues.summary: ExploreParams helper covers 3 of 4 shared parameters; nouns_per is parsed ad-hoc only in handleSemanticFieldExplore
evidence: parseExploreParams (line ~401) captures top, min_shared, and min_logdice into an ExploreParams record, but handleSemanticFieldExplore (line ~71-76) has its own separate try/catch block to parse nouns_per that is not covered by the helper and is absent from handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti — creating an asymmetry between the two sibling handlers.
issues.suggestion: Add nouns_per to ExploreParams (with a default of 30) and parse it inside parseExploreParams. Both handlers then receive a complete parameter object, and the ad-hoc try/catch in handleSemanticFieldExplore can be removed. Multi-seed can ignore nouns_per or the record can carry it without using it.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader primary constructor is a ~95-line monolith mixing parsing, validation, and relation construction without helper delegation
evidence: The private constructor (lines 97-192) sequentially performs: JSON root parsing, version validation, copulas-key rejection, relations-array null check, per-relation field extraction, position derivation, position range validation (with a multi-flag conditional), RelationConfig instantiation, duplicate-id check, and final list/map wrapping — all in one unbroken method body with only inline comments to delineate sections.
issues.suggestion: Extract at least two helpers: validateRoot(JSONObject root) throws IOException (covering version check and copulas rejection) and parseRelation(JSONObject relObj, int index) throws IOException (covering id/pattern extraction, position derivation, and validation). The constructor body then becomes a visible sequence of validate→parseRelations loop→assign, which a reader can scan in <10 lines.

root_causes: Incomplete local refactoring: helpers are introduced but not extended to cover all adjacent cases (ExploreParams, scoring phase constant), suggesting the refactoring was done under time pressure and stopped one step short.
Constructor-as-procedure pattern: the GrammarConfigLoader constructor treats itself as a validation+parsing script rather than a thin initializer delegating to named methods.
